reasoning: To reach a 4.1% YoY CPI at any point in 2025, inflation must rise from its latest reported rate of 3.0% in September 2025 to 4.1% by the end of the year. With only three months of reports remaining (October, November, December), this requires consecutive very high month-over-month prints (e.g., ~0.5% - 0.6%) to overcome base effects from late 2024. While delayed pass-through from historically high tariffs (15.8% as of mid-2025) and energy shocks create upside risk, the majority of expert forecasts (Wells Fargo, RBC, J.P. Morgan) project year-end inflation between 3.0% and 3.5%. Even the most hawkish institutional forecasts (Vanguard, Capital Economics) specifically cite a peak 'around 4.0%', suggesting that breaching the 4.1% threshold would fall at the very tail end of current expectations. A steep 1.1-point acceleration in just three months is probabilistically low, translating to roughly a 14% chance.
batch_queries: Will inflation reach more than 4% in 2025?
